formal_model_status.gap: The formal model names write-buffer commands in the command table and generic special-message paths, but does not define an executable write-buffer primitive or its buffer-full and clearing boundaries.

legacy_write_buffer_witnesses.summary: RAA dispatches write-buf-zero and write-buf-one through write-buf; write-buf appends 0 or 1 only when buffer-full? is false.

legacy_write_buffer_witnesses.input_clearing: stem-process-special-message wrapper clears input through zero-buf/zero-hig flow rather than a matching RAA-style input-process boundary
legacy_write_buffer_witnesses.summary: SEMSIM defines write-buf-zero and write-buf-one as appends, but stem-process-special-message wraps the selected operation in zero-hig and zero-buf, erasing the buffer after the append.

legacy_write_buffer_witnesses.input_clearing: write-buf-zero and write-buf-one clear smb and input channels
legacy_write_buffer_witnesses.summary: FSMSIM appends 0 or 1 to the buffer and clears self-mailbox plus input channels, without a matching buffer-full guard.

required_resolution_questions.summary: Decide whether write-buffer command messages execute on fixed recipient cells, stem cells only, or only through self-mailbox / command-buffer surfaces.
Decide whether write-buffer commands are ignored, rejected, preserve state, or report a status when the command buffer is full.
Decide whether write-buffer execution preserves the appended buffer, clears it like SEMSIM's stem wrapper, or clears only input/mail state.
Decide how write-buffer commands interact with the high rail and standard-signal command-buffer path.

as_boundary: Continue rejecting or preserving write-buffer commands at the existing claimed boundaries until a later ADR selects one source-backed execution semantics.
